mechanism_narrative: A 5-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening demand cycle that reduces corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.0 million units signals a weakening supply cycle that reduces new capacity and increases production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 3.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in real GDP below 2.5% signals a weakening demand cycle that reduces corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial jobless claims above 400,000 signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressure and reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signal a weakening supply cycle and a tightening labor market that reduces new capacity and increases production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in weakening supply and tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real GDP and a 2-month rise in initial jobless claims signal a weakening demand cycle and a tightening labor market that reduces corporate earnings growth and increases wage pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening demand and tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
